title: Stock and Flow
author: From: Thinking in Systems
content: A stock is the level of something (e.g., your portfolio value); a flow is the rate of change (e.g., contributions or withdrawals). Focus on the flows to change the stocks.
action: Action: Define the 'flow' that most influences your Roth growth.

title: Balancing vs. Reinforcing Loops
author: From: The Fifth Discipline
content: Reinforcing loops create exponential growth (good or bad); balancing loops provide stability and limits. Understand which one governs your current system.
action: Action: Identify the 'limit' in your current strategy—is it a balancing loop you need to break?

title: Systemic Delay
author: From: The Fifth Discipline
content: In any complex system, there is a time lag between action and result. Do not confuse a lack of immediate result with a lack of progress.
action: Action: Identify a 'long-game' strategy you are currently waiting on and reaffirm its logic.

title: Leverage Points
author: From: Thinking in Systems
content: Places within a complex system where a small shift in one thing can produce big changes in everything.
action: Action: If you could change one 'input' to your portfolio, which has the highest leverage?

title: The Boundary Constraint
author: From: Systems Thinking
content: A system is defined by its boundaries. When you feel stuck, it is usually because you are operating within a boundary you have the power to redefine.
action: Action: Question one 'rule' you have set for your own decision-making process.
